servinfo.title: Front Brake Pad Removal and Installation

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage front brake flexible hoses.
Never depress brake pedal during service operation shown below.
Check for leakage of brake fluid when removing front brake pads and repair front brake caliper if leakage is found.
Replace all front brake pads at the same time even if only one pad is defective.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove front wheel.
Remove front brake caliper pin bolts (1).
Remove front brake caliper (1) from front brake caliper carrier (2).

item.ptxt: Hang removed front brake caliper (1) with a wire hook (4) to prevent front brake flexible hose from bending, twisting excessively or being pulled.
Do not operate brake pedal with front brake pads removed.

s1.ptxt: Remove front brake pads (3) and pad springs (5) from front brake caliper carrier (2).

attention2.ptxt: Do not twist front brake flexible hose when installing front brake caliper.

s1.ptxt: Install pad springs (1) to front brake caliper carrier (2).
Fit shims (1) to inner front brake pad (3) and anti-noise shim (2) to outer front brake pad (4).
Install front brake pads (1) with shims to front brake caliper carrier (2) as shown in figure.

attention3.ptxt: Be sure to install inner front brake pad at inside of caliper carrier with its wear indicator (1) positioned upside.

s1.ptxt: Install front brake caliper and tighten front brake caliper pin bolts (1) to specified torque.
Check that brake system operates properly.
